title: Why Particle Physics Became a Victim of Its Own Success - Adam Brown
summary: Adam Brown discusses how particle physics has faced challenges as a field despite historical successes. The episode explores the constraints and difficulties that have emerged as the discipline has matured, presented as a critical perspective on the field's current situation.
writeup: Adam Brown talks with Dwarkesh Patel about how particle physics, after a run of major historical successes, now confronts structural difficulties as a mature field. The conversation frames those successes as a source of the present constraints, examining why progress has grown harder once the field accomplished so much. The takeaway is a critical perspective on the discipline's current situation rather than a celebration of past achievements.
